document.name: gan-harness-cli-showcase
document.title: GAN harness (CLI): Planner → Generator↔Evaluator contract negotiation → contract-graded build loop → PR
document.summary: Anthropic GAN-style long-running harness. A Planner writes a HIGH-LEVEL SPEC.md (no granular criteria); the Generator and a skeptical Evaluator then NEGOTIATE a rigid, browser-testable acceptance contract (contract.json, each criterion passes:false) before any code. The build loop generates, passes a deterministic build gate, and the Evaluator grades the running app against EACH contract criterion via Playwright MCP, updating passes flags; a progress.json agentic-memory log is read+appended every turn. Exits only when the build passes AND every contract criterion passes; opens a PR. interactive-cli family; GITHUB_TOKEN auto-injected.

intent.title: Redesign request
intent.default: Redesign the Nimbus Notes landing page (src/routes/+page.svelte plus a global stylesheet) into a polished, DISTINCTIVE product landing page with a coherent visual identity — strong typography hierarchy, deliberate color/spacing, a hero, the three feature cards, a pricing section, and a clear primary CTA. Plain SvelteKit + CSS only; keep `npm run build` passing; do NOT change package.json deps or routing.
intent.description: What the agents change in the repo.

planAgent.title: Planner agent
planAgent.description: PLAN phase agent (clones + writes SPEC). Same workspace-backend family.

criticAgent.title: Critic agent
criticAgent.description: EVALUATOR agent — negotiates the contract AND grades the build (drives Playwright MCP).

buildCommand.title: Build command
buildCommand.description: Build command the deterministic gate runs ('auto' = detect pnpm/npm/cargo/make).

previewCommand.title: Preview command
previewCommand.description: Preview/serve command the critic views ('auto' = detect npm/pnpm).

designPass.title: Two-pass design
designPass.description: Run the design-tokens+wireframe review BEFORE code ('false' to skip).

criticVotes.title: Critic votes
criticVotes.description: Independent Evaluator votes per build iteration; a criterion passes only if ALL votes agree (1 = single critic).

maxRestarts.title: Max restarts
maxRestarts.description: How many times the Evaluator may force a from-scratch restart (git reset --hard baseline) on fundamentally-broken work.

agentConfig.name: Planner
agentConfig.instructions:
You are the PLANNER for a coding redesign on a SvelteKit repo. FIRST clone the repo into /sandbox/work/repo by running EXACTLY this shell:
  cd /sandbox/work && rm -rf repo && git clone https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git repo && cd repo && git config user.name agent && git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local && git remote set-url origin https://github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git
Then READ the repo (src/routes/+page.svelte, package.json) and write a HIGH-LEVEL product + design spec to /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (ONLY that file; do NOT edit the repo). Keep it to product context, target audience, the desired mood/identity, and the FOUR design dimensions: design_quality, originality, craft, functionality. Deliberately stay HIGH-LEVEL: do NOT enumerate granular, implementation-level acceptance criteria — the Generator and the Evaluator negotiate the specific, testable criteria in the next phase. End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Redesign request:
" + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the landing page") + "

Repo to redesign: https://github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo
FIRST clone it into /sandbox/work/repo, authenticating with the GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable, by running this shell:
  cd /sandbox/work && rm -rf repo && git clone https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git repo && cd repo && git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local && git config user.name agent && git remote set-url origin https://github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git
Then READ the repo and write a HIGH-LEVEL product+design spec to /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (product context, mood/identity, the four design dimensions). Do NOT enumerate granular acceptance criteria (those are negotiated next). Do NOT edit the repo. End with a one-line summary." }

design.if: ${ (.approve_goal_spec.timedOut // false) == false and (.approve_goal_spec.approved // true) == true and ((.trigger.designPass // "true") != "false") }

agentConfig.name: Designer (Generator, two-pass)
agentConfig.instructions:
You are the GENERATOR doing the FIRST design pass, BEFORE writing any code (no CSS/HTML yet). Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. Produce a compact, OPINIONATED design system and commit to a distinctive direction (avoid generic 'AI slop' — no default purple-gradient-on-white-cards, no near-black + acid-green). Write TWO files at /sandbox/work (NOT in the repo):
1) /sandbox/work/design-tokens.json — STRICT JSON: {"palette":[{"name":"...","hex":"#..."}, ... 4-6 named colors], "typography":{"display":"font + role","body":"font + role"}, "spacing":"the scale/rhythm", "motif":"the one bold, justifiable aesthetic risk"}.
2) /sandbox/work/wireframe.txt — an ASCII wireframe of the page layout (hero, feature cards, pricing, CTA), top to bottom.
On later rounds READ /sandbox/work/design-review.json (the Evaluator's objections) and REVISE both files to address every objection. Do NOT edit the repo. End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Two-pass design (proposal). Intent: " + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the landing page") + "

Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md and, if present, /sandbox/work/design-review.json (prior objections). Write/revise /sandbox/work/design-tokens.json (4-6 named hex + display/body type + spacing + one bold motif) and /sandbox/work/wireframe.txt (ASCII layout). No code." }

agentConfig.name: Design lead (Evaluator, two-pass)
agentConfig.instructions: You are a SKEPTICAL design lead at a small studio reviewing a design PLAN before any code (do NOT open a browser; there is nothing to view yet). Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md, /sandbox/work/design-tokens.json and /sandbox/work/wireframe.txt. Judge ONLY on Originality and Craft: does the palette/typography/spacing form a coherent, DISTINCTIVE identity grounded in the product+audience, with at least one bold, justifiable aesthetic risk? REJECT generic library-default / 'AI slop' choices (ubiquitous purple gradients over white cards; near-black backgrounds with acid-green accents; default system fonts with no hierarchy). Write /sandbox/work/design-review.json as STRICT JSON: {"approved": <bool>, "feedback": "specific, actionable objections or what to change"}. Set approved:true ONLY when the direction is distinctive and coherent (not generic) — otherwise approved:false with concrete objections. End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Review the DESIGN PLAN (no code yet). Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md, design-tokens.json, wireframe.txt. Write /sandbox/work/design-review.json {approved, feedback}. Reject generic/AI-slop direction; approve only a distinctive, coherent one." }

with.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do [ -s /sandbox/work/design-review.json ] && break; sleep 1; done
python3 - <<'PYZZ'
import json,os
P='/sandbox/work/design-review.json'
try:
    d=json.load(open(P))
except Exception:
    print(json.dumps({'approved':False,'haveTokens':os.path.exists('/sandbox/work/design-tokens.json'),'error':'design-review.json missing/invalid'}));raise SystemExit
ap=d.get('approved')
if ap is None:
    txt=str(d.get('verdict') or d.get('overall_verdict') or d.get('summary') or d.get('feedback') or '').lower()
    ap=(('approv' in txt) or ('looks good' in txt) or ('lgtm' in txt) or ('accept' in txt)) and not (('reject' in txt) or ('revise' in txt) or ('not approv' in txt))
have=os.path.exists('/sandbox/work/design-tokens.json')
approved=bool(ap) and have
print(json.dumps({'approved':approved,'haveTokens':have}))
PYZZ

agentConfig.name: Proposer (Generator)
agentConfig.instructions: You are the GENERATOR in a contract NEGOTIATION, BEFORE writing any code. Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. Propose a concrete plan: break the redesign into a SMALL set of discrete, INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIABLE features. For EACH feature state (a) what you will implement, (b) EXACTLY how it can be verified in a browser (what to look for / interact with), and (c) a crisp acceptance criterion. On later rounds, READ /sandbox/work/contract-review.md (the Evaluator's objections) and REVISE your proposal to address every objection. Write your proposal to /sandbox/work/proposal.md (ONLY that file; do NOT edit the repo and do NOT write contract.json). End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Contract negotiation (proposal). Intent: " + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the landing page") + "\n\nRead /sandbox/work/SPEC.md and, if it exists, /sandbox/work/contract-review.md (the Evaluator's objections from the previous round). Write/revise /sandbox/work/proposal.md with discrete, browser-verifiable features + acceptance criteria per your instructions." }

agentConfig.name: Evaluator (Contract negotiation)
agentConfig.instructions: You are a HIGHLY SKEPTICAL EVALUATOR negotiating the acceptance contract for a redesign, BEFORE any code is written. You do NOT write code and you do NOT open a browser yet. Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md and /sandbox/work/proposal.md. Adversarially review the proposed features + acceptance criteria. Reject vague/unmeasurable/overly-broad criteria and ones that omit key edge cases (empty/error states, responsive/mobile layout, keyboard focus / accessibility, interactive elements actually working). Produce a FOCUSED contract — aim for roughly 8-15 of the MOST IMPORTANT, user-visible acceptance criteria (quality over quantity; don't pad with dozens of micro-checks, but don't drop important coverage either). Prefer PERCEPTUAL or STRUCTURAL criteria — the kind of check a human design reviewer makes by looking at and clicking the page (e.g. 'the hero headline is clearly the largest, most prominent text', 'the primary CTA is visually distinct and obviously clickable', 'the three feature cards sit in a row on desktop and stack cleanly on mobile', 'keyboard focus is visible on interactive elements'). AVOID brittle exact-number assertions (specific pixel sizes / hex / rgb values) where a perceptual description works; describe the INTENT. Write your objections + required changes to /sandbox/work/contract-review.md. THEN write the negotiated contract to /sandbox/work/contract.json as JSON of the form {"agreed": <boolean>, "criteria": [{"id":"c1","dimension":"design_quality|originality|craft|functionality","description":"the criterion","verify":"how a reviewer checks it in the browser","passes": false}, ...]}. Give each criterion a clear description + verify and a dimension when you can; every criterion starts with "passes": false. (A deterministic normalizer canonicalizes field names afterward, so focus on clear, testable description+verify rather than exact key names.) CONVERGENCE: set "agreed": true as soon as the criteria are specific, browser-testable, and cover the key cases — do NOT withhold agreement over minor polish; the build loop iterates on quality. Only keep "agreed": false if criteria are genuinely vague or missing important coverage. End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Negotiate the acceptance contract. Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md and /sandbox/work/proposal.md, write your objections to /sandbox/work/contract-review.md, and write/update /sandbox/work/contract.json (agreed + criteria, each passes:false) as STRICT JSON per your instructions. Be skeptical: set agreed:true ONLY when every criterion is specific, browser-verifiable, and covers edge cases." }

with.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do [ -s /sandbox/work/contract.json ] && break; sleep 1; done
python3 - <<'PYZZ'
import json,re
P='/sandbox/work/contract.json'
def dim(t):
    t=t.lower()
    if re.search(r'typograph|spacing|color|contrast|hierarch|whitespace|align|legib|craft',t): return 'craft'
    if re.search(r'distinct|original|custom|bold|unique|generic|template|slop|memorable',t): return 'originality'
    if re.search(r'identity|mood|cohesi|brand|aesthetic|palette|tone|overall|polish',t): return 'design_quality'
    return 'functionality'
try:
    raw=json.load(open(P))
except Exception:
    print(json.dumps({'agreed':False,'criteriaCount':0,'valid':False,'error':'contract.json missing/invalid'}));raise SystemExit
crit = raw.get('criteria') if isinstance(raw.get('criteria'),list) else []
norm=[]
for i,c in enumerate(crit):
    if not isinstance(c,dict): continue
    desc = c.get('description') or c.get('text') or c.get('criterion') or c.get('requirement') or c.get('name') or ''
    ver  = c.get('verify') or c.get('verification') or c.get('check') or c.get('how') or ''
    dd = c.get('dimension')
    if dd not in ('design_quality','originality','craft','functionality'): dd = dim((str(desc)+' '+str(ver)) or str(c.get('feature') or ''))
    norm.append({'id':str(c.get('id') or ('c'+str(i+1))),'dimension':dd,'description':str(desc),'verify':str(ver),'passes':bool(c.get('passes'))})
# Deterministic dedupe (count stays soft guidance): drop exact-normalized-text
# duplicates, and collapse redundant 'npm run build passes' criteria to one (the
# deterministic gate already enforces the build).
import re as _re
def _nk(s): return ' '.join(_re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9 ]',' ',str(s).lower()).split())
def _isbuild(s):
    t=str(s).lower()
    return ('npm run build' in t or 'run build' in t) and bool(_re.search(r'exit|complete|succeed|success|passes|builds? ok|build ok|builds? clean',t))
seen=set(); dd=[]; build_seen=False
for c in norm:
    k=_nk(c['description'])
    if k and k in seen: continue
    if _isbuild(c['description']):
        if build_seen: continue
        build_seen=True
    if k: seen.add(k)
    dd.append(c)
removed=len(norm)-len(dd); norm=dd
valid = len(norm)>=3 and all(x['description'] for x in norm)
agreed = bool(raw.get('agreed')) and valid
raw['criteria']=norm; raw['agreed']=agreed
json.dump(raw,open(P,'w'))
print(json.dumps({'agreed':agreed,'criteriaCount':len(norm),'deduped':removed,'valid':valid}))
PYZZ

refine.while: ${ ( (( ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS")) and (((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.meets_criteria // false)) == true) ) | not) and ((((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.stalled // false)) == true) | not) ) }

agentConfig.name: Generator
agentConfig.instructions: You are the GENERATOR. Implement the redesign in /sandbox/work/repo to satisfy the NEGOTIATED CONTRACT. GETTING UP TO SPEED (do this FIRST, every turn): read /sandbox/work/progress.json (the chronological log of prior iterations) and run `git -C /sandbox/work/repo log --oneline -10`. If /sandbox/work/design-tokens.json and /sandbox/work/wireframe.txt exist, treat them as the AUTHORITATIVE visual direction (color tokens, typography, spacing, the bold motif, layout) — implement THAT design; do not invent a different palette/type system. Then read /sandbox/work/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE acceptance criteria — each has id/description/verify/passes) and /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. Focus on the criteria whose "passes" is false. Edit src/routes/+page.svelte and a global stylesheet (src/routes/+layout.svelte importing app.css, or a <style> block) — plain SvelteKit + CSS, NO new dependencies, do NOT touch package.json or routing. You do NOT grade your own work: a deterministic build gate + an independent skeptical Evaluator (who VIEWS the rendered page and tests EACH contract criterion) evaluate every attempt. Each turn: address the build output AND the Evaluator's per-criterion feedback, make the most impactful improvement toward the FAILING criteria, dependencies are PRE-INSTALLED (node_modules present) so run the project's build yourself to verify before finishing, end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Redesign request: " + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the landing page") + "

Repo: /sandbox/work/repo. Authoritative acceptance criteria: /sandbox/work/contract.json. Spec: /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. Memory: /sandbox/work/progress.json (read it + git log FIRST)." + "

Your previous attempt:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(first attempt)") + "

Build gate (must end OBJECTIVE PASS):
" + ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") // "(not run yet)") + "

Evaluator per-criterion feedback (from the browser):
" + (try ((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.feedback // .summary // "(none)")) catch "(none)") + "

Make the build pass AND satisfy every FAILING contract criterion, then end with a brief summary." }

agentConfig.name: Critic
agentConfig.instructions:
You are an exacting, SKEPTICAL EVALUATOR with strong taste, independent of the builder and of any other evaluator; you do NOT edit the repo. GRADE the running app against the negotiated contract.

STEP 1 — start the app. The build already ran (node_modules + build output exist in /sandbox/work/repo). Start the preview server on port 4173 in the background and wait until it answers:
  cd /sandbox/work/repo && (nohup npm run preview -- --port 4173 --host 127.0.0.1 >/tmp/preview.log 2>&1 &) ; for i in $(seq 1 40); do curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:4173/ >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done ; tail -5 /tmp/preview.log
If preview never answers, fall back to dev on the same port and wait the same way.

STEP 2 — inspect with the Playwright MCP browser tools (server 'playwright'): browser_navigate to http://127.0.0.1:4173/ , browser_snapshot, browser_take_screenshot (filename 'critic-shot.png' -> /sandbox/work/critic-shot.png). Resize the viewport (desktop/tablet/mobile) and browser_click the nav / primary CTA, re-snapshotting, to test interactivity + responsiveness. Use the BROWSER — do not write your own screenshot scripts.

IGNORE churn in package-lock.json, node_modules/, .svelte-kit/, build/. Judge ONLY the redesign source (src/) and the rendered UI.

STEP 3 — read /sandbox/work/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE criteria), /sandbox/work/SPEC.md, /sandbox/work/design-tokens.json (the agreed visual direction), and the source.

STEP 4 — grade the rendered app against EACH criterion. Decide passes true/false based on what you ACTUALLY SAW / INTERACTED WITH (use the criterion's "verify"). DEFAULT to passes:false unless the evidence is clear. Generic or merely 'fine' work does NOT pass.

STEP 5 — WRITE your verdict to /sandbox/work/verdict-0.json as STRICT JSON with EXACTLY this shape: {"criteria": [{"id": "c1", "passes": <bool>, "feedback": "concrete browser evidence"}, ...], "allPass": <bool>, "score": <0-100>, "meets_criteria": <same as allPass>, "recommend_restart": <bool — TRUE ONLY if the work is FUNDAMENTALLY broken (build/page does not render, core layout entirely absent) so starting over beats patching; else false>, "feedback": "overall summary of what still fails"}. Write ONLY verdict-0.json (do NOT edit contract.json — a deterministic aggregator combines the votes). After writing it, stop the server (pkill -f vite 2>/dev/null; pkill -f 'npm run' 2>/dev/null) and end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Grade the redesigned app in /sandbox/work/repo against EACH criterion in /sandbox/work/contract.json by VIEWING + interacting with it in the browser (Playwright MCP), then WRITE /sandbox/work/verdict-0.json (per-criterion passes + allPass + meets_criteria + recommend_restart). Follow your instructions exactly." + "

Preview command for THIS run: " + (.trigger.previewCommand // "auto") }

agentConfig.name: Critic (vote 2)
agentConfig.instructions:
You are an exacting, SKEPTICAL EVALUATOR with strong taste, independent of the builder and of any other evaluator; you do NOT edit the repo. GRADE the running app against the negotiated contract.

STEP 1 — start the app. The build already ran (node_modules + build output exist in /sandbox/work/repo). Start the preview server on port 4173 in the background and wait until it answers:
  cd /sandbox/work/repo && (nohup npm run preview -- --port 4173 --host 127.0.0.1 >/tmp/preview.log 2>&1 &) ; for i in $(seq 1 40); do curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:4173/ >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done ; tail -5 /tmp/preview.log
If preview never answers, fall back to dev on the same port and wait the same way.

STEP 2 — inspect with the Playwright MCP browser tools (server 'playwright'): browser_navigate to http://127.0.0.1:4173/ , browser_snapshot, browser_take_screenshot (filename 'critic-shot.png' -> /sandbox/work/critic-shot.png). Resize the viewport (desktop/tablet/mobile) and browser_click the nav / primary CTA, re-snapshotting, to test interactivity + responsiveness. Use the BROWSER — do not write your own screenshot scripts.

IGNORE churn in package-lock.json, node_modules/, .svelte-kit/, build/. Judge ONLY the redesign source (src/) and the rendered UI.

STEP 3 — read /sandbox/work/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE criteria), /sandbox/work/SPEC.md, /sandbox/work/design-tokens.json (the agreed visual direction), and the source.

STEP 4 — grade the rendered app against EACH criterion. Decide passes true/false based on what you ACTUALLY SAW / INTERACTED WITH (use the criterion's "verify"). DEFAULT to passes:false unless the evidence is clear. Generic or merely 'fine' work does NOT pass.

STEP 5 — WRITE your verdict to /sandbox/work/verdict-1.json as STRICT JSON with EXACTLY this shape: {"criteria": [{"id": "c1", "passes": <bool>, "feedback": "concrete browser evidence"}, ...], "allPass": <bool>, "score": <0-100>, "meets_criteria": <same as allPass>, "recommend_restart": <bool — TRUE ONLY if the work is FUNDAMENTALLY broken (build/page does not render, core layout entirely absent) so starting over beats patching; else false>, "feedback": "overall summary of what still fails"}. Write ONLY verdict-1.json (do NOT edit contract.json — a deterministic aggregator combines the votes). After writing it, stop the server (pkill -f vite 2>/dev/null; pkill -f 'npm run' 2>/dev/null) and end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Grade the redesigned app in /sandbox/work/repo against EACH criterion in /sandbox/work/contract.json by VIEWING + interacting with it in the browser (Playwright MCP), then WRITE /sandbox/work/verdict-1.json (per-criterion passes + allPass + meets_criteria + recommend_restart). Follow your instructions exactly." + "

Preview command for THIS run: " + (.trigger.previewCommand // "auto") }

artifacts.title: Rendered redesign (Playwright critic screenshot)
artifacts.description: Full-page screenshot the critic captured in-browser via the Playwright MCP server.

artifacts.title: Negotiated acceptance contract
artifacts.description: The Generator↔Evaluator negotiated contract.json (testable criteria + per-criterion passes flags).

with.command: cd /sandbox/work/repo && git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local && git config user.name 'workflow-builder agent' && BR=redesign-$(date +%s) && git checkout -b $BR && git add -A && (git commit -m 'Redesign Nimbus Notes landing page (workflow-builder plan/generate/critic)' || echo nothing-to-commit) && git push https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git HEAD:$BR && PR=$(curl -sS -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" -H 'Accept: application/vnd.github+json' https://api.github.com/repos/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo/pulls -d "{\"title\":\"Redesign landing page (automated)\",\"head\":\"$BR\",\"base\":\"main\",\"body\":\"Automated redesign via the workflow-builder plan/generate/critic loop; a Playwright critic inspected the rendered UI.\"}") && echo "$PR" | python3 -c 'import sys,json;d=json.load(sys.stdin);print("PR_URL="+str(d.get("html_url") or d.get("message") or d))'

set.terminalState: ${ if ( ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS")) and (((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.meets_criteria // false)) == true) ) then "satisfied" elif (((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.stalled // false)) == true) then "stalled_no_progress" else "max_iterations_reached" end }

artifacts.title: Coding redesign result
artifacts.description: Build + critic verdicts, iterations, PR output.
